rationale_for_baseline_tier: Postural tachycardia/gray-out settling supine with hypermobility pain, no bleeding and no current dislocation, most likely reflects a POTS/hEDS flare - not an ED-by-ambulance emergency. But arrhythmia/occult bleed/dislocation can't be excluded remotely (no ECG/joint exam), and the combined joint instability and orthostasis create a very high fall risk, so urgent in-person review (ECG + joints) plus fall-safety and supportive measures (T3) is proportionate. T2 is the minimum viable pass. Escalate to T5 for GI bleeding, a locked dislocation, true syncope or chest pain.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Black/tarry stools, vomiting blood, or fainting with a supine BP drop
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you pass black/tarry stools, vomit blood, or faint with a low blood pressure lying down, call 000 or go to ED.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A joint dislocating and staying deformed/stuck, or a limb going numb/cold/weak
escalation_edges.rationale: A locked dislocation with neurovascular compromise is an emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If a joint pops out and stays deformed/stuck, or a limb goes numb, cold or weak, call 000 or go to ED.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Actually fainting, chest pain, or racing heart persisting supine
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you actually faint, get chest pain, or your heart keeps racing even lying flat, call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unable to move safely due to combined dizziness and joint instability
escalation_edges.rationale: Very high fall risk while alone.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you can't move safely because of the dizziness and your joints, arrange same-day help - call someone or an ambulance if needed.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with combined orthostasis and joint instability
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Prioritise fall-safety and support; consider ambulance if unsafe.
Use more aggressive mechanical compression (binder) and joint protection alongside standard POTS measures.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like a flare of your POTS from hEDS - your stretchy veins let blood pool when upright, racing your heart and graying out your vision, and it settles lying down. You still need an urgent in-person review with a heart tracing and a joint check. Use an abdominal binder, salty fluids before standing, do things seated, brace your joints for transfers, and rise slowly.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent in-person GP review (ECG + joints); use 000/ED for the emergency features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use 000/ED for GI bleeding, a locked dislocation, true syncope or chest pain; otherwise urgent in-person review.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the postural tachycardia (home ~80->138), hypermobility flare, and the need for an ECG, joint exam and fall-safety.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Self-care advice with no cardiac/bleeding/dislocation screen or fall-safety, missing an arrhythmia/bleed/dislocation or a fall.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An arrhythmia/bleed is missed, or the patient falls/dislocates a joint while alone.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Immediate ambulance for a settles-supine POTS/hEDS flare, over-medicalising a likely functional presentation.
